Contact Profile: Mr. Peterson has been writing articles on various technology-related subjects for years. You can find a number of his articles at eSecurityGuy.com. He's been on Clear Channel radio for more than three years, with his own show since December 2004. Stories that interest Mr. Peterson is anything dealing with practical applications of technology. This includes new products or services and even some of the staples. He covers everything from recreational vehicles and barbecues, through computers and NASA. He enjoy the review-and-comment on the news segment of my radio show. He also enjoys guests who are lucid and can talk about various aspects of their products/services. He refers to the following sites: SlashDot.net, Google, news.yahoo.com, CNET.com, USA Today, Popular Science/Electronics, Wall Street Journal, etc. He's found PR firms to be very helpful. Biggest tip: If you've got a client who's interested in being a radio guest, make sure they can talk off the cuff. It also helps if they can be easily understood over the phone. Usually, the best way to contact the show is via e-mail: producer@tech-talk-with-craig-peterson.com.  They like photos/graphics/artwork, only upon request.

Contact Profile: <From Bulldog> Goldstein doesn't have a beat. "I'm the PD, which means I manage the people who manage the people who have beats," she says. "I don't entertain pitches.
